assertions.text: Vehicle was traveling at approximately 100 km/h in the left lane.
The vehicle occupied the left lane prior to the event.
The car directly ahead of the vehicle suddenly decelerated (slowed down) before the collision.
The driver applied brakes for several seconds before impact.
The front vehicle slowed, then the driver braked for several seconds, then the driver's vehicle struck the front vehicle with its front end.
The vehicle impacted the rear of the car in front with its front end.
There was a single impact between the vehicles (the front of the following vehicle hitting the rear of the lead vehicle).
The collision could not be avoided despite braking, implying a non-trivial impact (not dismissed as a light tap).

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry shows the vehicle speed ranged from [253.0 km/h first sample](T2) to [203.0 km/h last pre-impact sample](T2) in the pre-crash window and the EDR vehicle speed limit is [113.0 km/h](T1), so the claim of ~[100 km/h](T1) is contradicted by recorded speed.
Lane position is not available in the telemetry channels; no channel indicates lane occupancy in the provided data so the assertion is unverifiable.

There is no direct sensor channel reporting the lead vehicle's behavior; telemetry shows vehicle deceleration (speed dropped from [253.0 km/h](T2) to [203.0 km/h](T2)) but this alone cannot distinguish whether the lead vehicle suddenly slowed, so the claim is unverifiable from available channels.
Brake channel statistics show the brake was turned on at [t=-4.5 s](T2) and the fraction of samples with brake on is [0.9](T2), indicating brakes were applied for several seconds before impact.

Sequence requires evidence of the lead vehicle slowing then driver braking then front-to-rear impact. Telemetry confirms the driver braked (brake on at [t=-4.5 s](T2)) and a frontal crash pulse occurred with longitudinal delta-V peaking at [ -89.0 km/h at t=0.14 s](T2), but there is no direct data about the lead vehicle's prior deceleration or explicit timestamps tying its slowdown before the braking, so the full sequence is unverifiable.

EDR crash-pulse shows a large negative longitudinal delta-V with max of [ -88.55 km/h at 140.0 ms](T1) and cumulative delta-v final value near [ -86.0 km/h](T2), consistent with a forward-facing (frontal) impact where V1 struck an object ahead with its front end.
The EDR summary reports [num_events_recorded: 1](T1) and the delta-V crash pulse is a single pulse with peak at [ -88.55 km/h at 140.0 ms](T1), supporting a single impact.

The recorded longitudinal delta-V magnitude is large (EDR summary max delta-V long [ -88.55 km/h](T1) and compute_stats peak ~[ -89.0 km/h](T2)), which indicates a substantial collision rather than a light tap, supporting the claim that braking could not avoid a non-trivial impact.

result.rationale: Telemetry shows the vehicle was braking for several seconds before a single, large-magnitude frontal impact (neg. longitudinal delta-V ~[-88.55 km/h](T1)), so the core claims about braking and a non-trivial front-to-rear collision are supported; lane occupancy and lead-vehicle behavior are not recorded.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
